headline: US Shale Drillers Seen Lifting Crude Output on Hormuz-Driven Price Rally
summary: (Bloomberg) -- US shale drillers are expected to follow President Donald Trump’s call for higher oil production — but not just because he said so. The 72% surge in crude prices since the US and Israel commenced attacks on Iran roughly five weeks ago is incentive enough to compel American oil executives to ramp up output, according to observers as diverse as Citigroup Inc. and Enverus Inc.Shale explorers require oil prices somewhere between $62 and $70 a barrel to turn a profit on new wells, acco

headline: Jamie Dimon Maps JPMorgan’s Risk Playbook And AI Spending Priorities
summary: Jamie Dimon has released his annual letter to JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) shareholders, outlining how the bank is thinking about major structural risks. The letter highlights concerns around geopolitics, persistent inflation, and potential regulatory changes. Dimon also details how artificial intelligence could reshape the firm and the broader financial system. New programs include a $1.5 trillion Security and Resiliency Initiative and the American Dream Initiative focused on U.S. economic...

headline: Bank of America (BAC) Looks Strong Ahead of Q1 Results but the Rate Tailwind Is Fading
summary: Bank of America (BAC) heads into Q1 results, expected on April 15, on a strong footing, supported by still-resilient net interest income (NII) and solid underlying fundamentals. However, that support is beginning to weaken as expectations shift toward Federal Reserve rate cuts, raising questions about how long the rate-driven tailwind can last.
